content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be re-framed as layered "agent communities" where each individual memory chunk acts as an independent curious micro-agent that actively votes on which new information the core agent should prioritize querying for learning. Curiosity-driven micro-agents in long-term memory only activate when they encounter unresolved ambiguity in new inputs, surfacing outdated memory traces to be updated via active sampling rather than leaving all sample selection to the core agent.
